---
title: "Building My Personal AI Utilization System"
date: 2026-03-12
description: "A practical architecture for using AI tools in research, learning, and software development."
toc: true
---

<div class="language-toggle" role="group" aria-label="Language switch"><a class="language-toggle__link is-active" href="/articles/building-my-personal-ai-utilization-system/en.html" lang="en">EN</a><a class="language-toggle__link" href="/articles/building-my-personal-ai-utilization-system/ko.html" lang="ko">KO</a></div>

# Motivation

::: {.section-lead}
AI tools are powerful, but using them individually quickly becomes messy.
:::

A typical workflow looks like this:

::: {.workflow-pipeline}
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">ChatGPT</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Perplexity</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">NotebookLM</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Drive</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">repeat</span>
:::

Over time:

* context becomes fragmented
* knowledge spreads across tools
* switching between AIs creates friction
* long-term work loses structure

The limitation is not the capability of AI.

::: {.highlight-box}
The real problem is the lack of architecture.
:::

# Goal

::: {.section-lead}
Design a system where multiple AI tools work together instead of being used independently.
:::

This system should support:

* studying university courses
* managing research materials
* writing long documents
* building software projects
* organizing knowledge over time

The focus is practical workflow, not theoretical design.

# Key Idea

::: {.section-lead}
Assign clear roles to different tools.
:::

```text
Search       → Perplexity
Store        → Zotero
Analyze      → NotebookLM
Organize     → Gemini
Design       → Claude
Build        → ChatGPT / Codex
Code State   → GitHub
Memory       → Google Drive
Control      → gws CLI
```

::: {.highlight-box}
Each tool does one job well.
:::

# Philosophy

<ol class="ordered-list">
  <li>
    <span class="ordered-list__title">AI assists thinking — it does not replace it</span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="ordered-list__title">AI is a cognitive tool</span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="ordered-list__title">Working systems &gt; perfect systems</span>
  </li>
</ol>

# Practical Constraints

::: {.section-lead}
AI workflow tooling is still immature.
:::

* no universal standard
* fragmented ecosystems
* high setup cost

And a common trap:

::: {.highlight-box}
You spend more time building the system than using it.
:::

This architecture is designed to avoid that.

# Core Architecture

This system is built around one simple idea:

::: {.highlight-box}
Google Drive = shared memory  
Gemini CLI = memory operator (Librarian)
:::

# Architecture Overview

This is not a linear pipeline.

It is an **input/output system centered on memory**.

## Diagram

<div class="text-diagram" role="img" aria-label="Architecture overview diagram.">
<pre class="text-diagram__pre">                   INPUTS
      PDFs / notes / docs / course files / drafts
                         |
                         v
              [ Gemini CLI (via gws CLI) ]
              - classify
              - rename
              - route
              - update memory
              - extract dates
                         |
                         v
                [ Google Drive Memory Layer ]
                         |
            +------------+------------+
            |                         |
            v                         v
    [ Knowledge Tools ]       [ Execution Tools ]
    - NotebookLM              - Claude
    - Perplexity              - ChatGPT / Codex
    - Zotero (manual)         - Claude Code
            |                         |
            v                         v
    Analysis / Search        Design / Build / Code
            \                         /
             \                       /
              +---------+-----------+
                        |
                        v
                      OUTPUTS
                 Google Drive / GitHub</pre>
</div>

# How It Works

## 1. Input → Gemini

::: {.section-lead}
All raw inputs go through Gemini CLI.
:::

* lecture files
* PDFs
* notes
* drafts

Gemini:

* reads content
* renames files
* classifies
* places them correctly
* updates context

## 2. Google Drive = Memory

::: {.section-lead}
Drive stores:
:::

* documents
* course materials
* project files
* AI context

This is what gives the system **continuity**.

## 3. AI Tools Consume Context

::: {.section-lead}
Different tools read from the same memory.
:::

### Knowledge side

* NotebookLM → document analysis
* Perplexity → search
* Zotero → paper archive (manual, source of truth)

### Execution side

* Claude → reasoning / design
* ChatGPT / Codex → implementation
* Claude Code → repo interaction

## 4. Output → System

Outputs are written back:

* Drive → knowledge
* GitHub → code

# Minimal Setup

::: {.section-lead}
You do NOT need everything.
:::

## Minimal Working System

```text
Google Drive
Gemini CLI
gws CLI
```

This alone gives you:

* file organization
* persistent memory
* reusable context

## Optional Tools

```text
Perplexity → search
NotebookLM → analysis
Claude → reasoning
ChatGPT / Codex → coding
GitHub → code state
Zotero → research archive
```

Add gradually.

## Key Insight

::: {.highlight-box}
The architecture matters more than the tools.
:::

# Google Drive Folder Architecture

```text
AI_OS/
├── University/
├── Projects/
└── Librarian/
```

# Librarian (Global System Context)

```text
Librarian/
    architecture.md
    routing.md
    decisions.md
```

Defines:

* system structure
* tool routing rules
* design decisions

# librarian_memory (Local Context)

Each course/project has its own:

```text
librarian_memory/
```

## Standard Files

```text
librarian_memory/
    current_task.md
    brief.md
    handoff.md
```

## current_task.md

Tracks active work.

```text
Working on:
Exercise B

Next:
Proof by induction

Deadline:
March 16
```

## brief.md

Stores key facts.

```text
Course: CAS2101
Midterm: April 21
Final: June 16
```

## handoff.md

Maintains continuity.

```text
Completed:
1–3

Remaining:
4–5

Notes:
proof structure 중요
```

## Why This Matters

Without this:

::: {.highlight-box}
every AI session resets
:::

With this:

::: {.highlight-box}
persistent cross-AI memory
:::

# 🔒 Librarian Control Policy (Critical)

The following folders are **AI-managed**:

```text
Librarian/
*/librarian_memory/
```

## Rule

::: {.highlight-box}
**Do NOT edit these manually.**
:::

Instead:

```text
"Update current_task.md to reflect new task"
```

## Why

These folders are:

::: {.highlight-box}
**system memory + AI context layer**
:::

Manual edits can break consistency.

## Principle

::: {.highlight-box}
**User controls intent
Gemini controls memory**
:::

# Gemini = Librarian

Gemini manages:

* file organization
* naming
* routing
* context updates
* summaries
* schedule extraction

# Example Workflows

## 1. Course Material Automation

::: {.workflow-pipeline}
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Download</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Gemini</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">auto organize + context update</span>
:::

## 2. Project Knowledge Management

```text
Code → GitHub
Docs → Drive (Gemini managed)
```

## 3. Research Workflow

::: {.workflow-pipeline}
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Perplexity</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Zotero</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">NotebookLM</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Claude</span>
:::

Zotero = original source

## 4. Shared Context Across AIs

All tools read:

```text
librarian_memory/
```

→ shared memory

## 5. Schedule Automation

::: {.workflow-pipeline}
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Document</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Gemini</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Calendar + memory update</span>
:::

# Flexibility & Evolution

::: {.section-lead}
This system is **loosely coupled**.
:::

You can:

* replace tools
* simplify structure
* customize workflows

## Context is Portable

All important state lives in:

```text
Drive + markdown files
```

So you can:

```text
move context → change tools → continue work
```

## Key Idea

::: {.highlight-box}
**Tools are replaceable
Context is persistent**
:::

# Interoperability (MCP)

This system aligns with emerging standards like:

::: {.highlight-box}
**Model Context Protocol (MCP)**
:::

Which enables:

* shared context
* tool interoperability
* external system integration

# Environment Setup (Guideline)

## Identity Layer

```text
Google
GitHub
OpenAI
Anthropic
Perplexity
```

👉 Use Google as unified login

## Development

```text
Git
VS Code
Node / Python
Terminal
```

## Core Integration

```text
gcloud CLI
gws CLI
Gemini CLI
Google Drive
```

## Setup Principle

::: {.highlight-box}
**Do not copy blindly.
Adapt to your environment.**
:::

# Quick Start

```text
1. Create folder structure
2. Add librarian_memory files
3. Install Gemini CLI + gws
4. Run first file
```

# Adapting the System

::: {.section-lead}
You don’t have to build this alone.
:::

## Use AI

```text
Give this article or repo to an AI
→ ask it to adapt the system
```

## Example

```text
"Adapt this to my Mac + Python workflow"
"Simplify this to minimal setup"
```

## Why This Works

Because the system is:

```text
modular + loosely coupled + context-driven
```

# Conclusion

::: {.section-lead}
AI tools alone create fragmentation.
:::

This system turns them into:

::: {.highlight-box}
**a structured, persistent working environment**
:::

Result:

* shared context
* organized knowledge
* reduced friction

# Repository

::: {.repository-card}
[ai-utilization-system](https://github.com/smkgenesis/ai-utilization-system)
:::
